title: VerificationReport
description: Output of `verify-bib` and `verify-ref`: the axis (a) reference-identity report (D9, D16). Each entry carries the reference-level verdict (verified, mismatch, unresolvable, inconclusive) AND the full set of per-source outcomes (confirmed, negative, source_error) that produced it. The per-source outcomes are mandatory, never summarized away: the clean-negative versus source_error distinction is what stops a downed index from being read as evidence of fabrication, and it is the only way a reader can audit why a verdict came out as it did. Only 'unresolvable' and 'mismatch' are refusal-grade; 'inconclusive' is NEVER refusal-grade. Each entry also reports the axis (b) publication status and the axis (d) accessibility of the same reference, because a reference can be perfectly verified on axis (a) and still be retracted on axis (b).

protocol_version.description: Version of the D9 decision rules in force for this report. Pinned so a replayed run is judged by the same rules that produced the original verdicts.

run_id.description: Provenance run this report belongs to, joining it to provenance-event.schema.json events.

generated_at.description: Caller-supplied RFC 3339 timestamp. Omitted in replay-determinism comparisons.

path.description: Path of the .bib file when kind is 'bib'.

reference.description: The DOI or title string when kind is 'reference'.

sources_queried.description: Connectors this run was configured to query, in query order.

entry.description: One reference: its axis (a) verdict with every per-source outcome retained, plus the axis (b) status and axis (d) accessibility of the same reference.

key.description: BibTeX citation key, or a stable index key for a single-reference run.

refusal_grade.description: True only for 'unresolvable' and 'mismatch'. Refusal-grade consumers (citation audit, compile gate, commit hook) may act ONLY when this is true. 'inconclusive' is never refusal-grade, and neither is 'verified'.

reason.description: Human-readable justification naming the D9 precedence rule that fired.

source_outcomes.description: One outcome per queried source. Mandatory and never elided: this is the audit trail behind the verdict, and the confirmed / negative / source_error split is what keeps a downed index from being read as fabrication evidence.

tally.description: Counts of the per-source outcomes, the direct inputs to the D9 precedence.

best_match.description: The best-matching record found across sources, in CSL-JSON per record.schema.json, or null when nothing resolved. Validated in full against record.schema.json; kept open here so the two schemas cannot drift out of sync.

disagreements.description: Sources that resolved the reference but disagreed beyond thresholds while the verdict was still 'verified' on a two-confirmation majority. D9 requires that a lone bad index is logged, never allowed to force a refusal-grade verdict.

status.description: Axis (b) publication status of this reference, reported independently of axis (a).

accessibility.description: Axis (d) accessibility of this reference: what evidence depth was even possible.

referenceClaim.description: The reference AS CLAIMED by the bib entry or the user, before any source resolution. Kept verbatim so a mismatch can be shown as claimed versus found.

authors.description: Author surnames as claimed, in order.

entry_type.description: BibTeX entry type as written, for example 'article' or 'inproceedings'.

raw.description: The raw .bib entry text, when the input was a bib file.

sourceOutcome.description: What one queried source returned. 'confirmed': the source resolved the reference and the metadata matched within thresholds. 'negative': the query succeeded and returned no match (a clean negative). 'source_error': timeout, rate limit, 5xx, or network failure, so no clean answer was obtained and a clean negative CANNOT be asserted.

matched_id.description: Identifier of the record the source resolved to, when it resolved anything.

matched_record.description: The record the source resolved to, in CSL-JSON per record.schema.json, or null.

title_similarity.description: rapidfuzz normalized-title similarity between the claimed and the resolved title, 0.0 to 1.0.

year_delta.description: Resolved year minus claimed year. Null when either year is unknown.

surname_overlap.description: Whether the first-author surname overlaps between claimed and resolved.

resolved.description: True when the source returned a candidate record at all, independent of whether that candidate matched within thresholds. A source that resolves the identifier but disagrees beyond thresholds is outcome 'negative' with resolved true and mismatch_reasons populated; that combination is what the D9 precedence reads as evidence for 'mismatch' rather than for 'unresolvable'. A source that cleanly found nothing is outcome 'negative' with resolved false.

mismatch_reasons.description: Which threshold checks failed for a resolving but disagreeing source.

error.description: Required when outcome is 'source_error', forbidden otherwise. The presence of a typed error is what marks the answer as dirty rather than clean.

response_hash.description: Hash of the snapshot that backs this outcome, tying it to snapshot.schema.json.

statusBlock.description: Axis (b) fields carried per entry. The full axis (b) report shape lives in status-report.schema.json; this is the per-entry projection of it.

checked.description: False when no status source could be consulted, in which case verdict defaults to 'current' only if a source cleanly reported no notices; a status that could not be checked is reported with checked false and MUST NOT be presented as a clean 'current'.

doi.description: DOI of the notice itself (the retraction notice or correction), not of the retracted work.

accessibilityBlock.description: Axis (d) fields carried per entry, derived from the OA cascade (Unpaywall, then arXiv, then PMC).

cascade.description: Which OA locations were tried, in order, and what each returned.

summary.description: Counts derived from entries. Every axis (a) verdict and every per-source outcome is tallied, so a reader sees at a glance how much of the report rests on dirty evidence.

refusal_grade.description: Number of entries a refusal-grade consumer may act on: the sum of 'unresolvable' and 'mismatch'.

thresholds.description: The calibrated D9 thresholds actually used for this run (see core/CALIBRATION.md). Emitted in the report so a verdict can never be read without the rule that produced it.

min_confirmations.description: Confirmations required for 'verified'. D9 sets this to 2.

identityVerdict.description: Axis (a) reference-identity verdict, exactly four states per D9. 'verified': at least min_confirmations sources confirmed. 'mismatch': a source resolves the identifier but the metadata disagrees beyond thresholds. 'unresolvable': ALL queried sources returned a clean negative and none confirmed; the only refusal-grade 'likely fabricated' state. 'inconclusive': evidence too thin or too dirty to decide (exactly one confirmation, or any source_error); NEVER refusal-grade.

sourceOutcomeVerdict.description: Per-source outcome, exactly three states. A source_error must never be counted as a negative.

statusVerdict.description: Axis (b) publication-status verdict, exactly four states.

accessibilityVerdict.description: Axis (d) accessibility verdict, exactly three states.

sourceName.description: Connector name. Known values: openalex, crossref, datacite, semantic_scholar, arxiv, pubmed, unpaywall, opencitations.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 content hash, hex encoded, optionally prefixed with 'sha256:'.

versions.description: Component versions that determine the output, per D15. Identical snapshot plus identical configuration plus identical versions must yield byte-identical JSON.
